pi_items.verified_value: Hazardous - produces ammonium nitrate, exothermic reaction, releases toxic ammonia and nitric acid fumes
pi_items.source: Chemical reactivity research and safety literature (https://www.chemicals.co.uk/blog/the-surprising-reaction-of-nitric-acid-ammonia, NCBI, Penn State research)

pi_items.verified_value: Hazardous - produces acetone imine (volatile and flammable), potential formation of other compounds under certain conditions
pi_items.source: Harvard ADS, Chemistry forums, PubChem, chemical research literature

pi_items.verified_value: Highly hazardous - violent exothermic reaction producing toxic and flammable vapors, documented explosion incidents

privileged_info_summary: The task requires analysis of three real chemicals using CAMEO Chemicals reactivity tool: Anhydrous Ammonia (UN 1005, CAS 7664-41-7), Nitric Acid >70% (UN 2031, CAS 7697-37-2), and Acetone (UN 1090, CAS 67-64-1). All three pairwise combinations are documented as hazardous: (1) Ammonia + Nitric Acid produces an exothermic reaction forming ammonium nitrate with toxic fume release; (2) Ammonia + Acetone forms acetone imine, a volatile flammable compound; (3) Nitric Acid + Acetone undergoes a violent exothermic reaction with documented explosion incidents. The facility inventory data is synthetic but uses real chemicals with correct UN and CAS numbers. CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/) is a verified real tool by NOAA and EPA.
